concentration.headline: Primate city dominance — one city overwhelms all others
concentration.body: Addis Ababa accounts for a striking 38.2% of tracked urban population across 142 cities in Ethiopia. This level of primacy (38%) is characteristic of rapidly urbanising or historically centralised economies where one city concentrates political, economic and cultural power.

gravity.headline: Dense gravitational cluster — Addis Ababa & Nazrēt
gravity.body: The strongest gravitational pull exists between Addis Ababa and Nazrēt (80 km apart, combined population 6,028,000). Cities form a tight gravitational cluster with top pairs averaging 102 km apart. This dense sub-regional network often functions as a single megalopolitan economy.

narration.synthesis: Ethiopia exhibits strong primate city dominance: Addis Ababa holds 38.2% of tracked urban population — a striking concentration. The gravity network confirms this: connections involving Addis Ababa dominate, with Addis Ababa → Nazrēt among secondary links. This pattern is characteristic of historically centralised or rapidly urbanising economies.
